description: Improve unit test coverage across the platform by filling genuine gaps in critical code paths. Every change adds tests for untested logic, strengthens imprecise assertions, or makes tests more idiomatic. No lateral changes to existing passing tests.

userStories.title: Add oversized IIFE rejection test to discovery-load.test.ts and fix try/catch patterns
userStories.description: As a platform developer, I want the IIFE size limit (5MB) in loadPluginFromDir to be tested, and the existing throwing tests to use idiomatic expect().toThrow() patterns, so that security boundaries have regression coverage and tests follow codebase conventions.
acceptanceCriteria: New test creates a temp plugin with an IIFE file exceeding 5MB and verifies loadPluginFromDir throws with an error message containing 'exceeding the 5MB limit'
The three existing tests that use try/catch + expect(caught).toBeDefined() (lines 72-107) are rewritten to use expect(async () => ...).toThrow() with message matchers
Error message content is verified in each throwing test (not just that it throws)
All existing tests continue to pass
userStories.notes: File: platform/mcp-server/src/discovery-load.test.ts. The source is discovery.ts lines 117-132 for the size check. The existing pattern for throwing tests in the codebase is expect(() => fn()).toThrow(/pattern/) — see manifest-schema.test.ts for examples. For the oversized IIFE test: create a temp dir with a valid opentabs-plugin.json and a dist/adapter.iife.js that is >5MB (use a string of repeated characters). The loadPluginFromDir function is async so use expect(async () => await loadPluginFromDir(...)).toThrow(). Follow the same temp dir pattern already used in the file (mkdtempSync, afterAll cleanup).

userStories.title: Add writeExecFile failure test to execute-script.test.ts
userStories.description: As a platform developer, I want the execute-script handler to have its writeExecFile failure path tested, so that error propagation behavior is verified for all steps of the multi-step handler.
acceptanceCriteria: New test where writeExecFile mock rejects — verifies the handler rejects with the same error
New test verifies the handler's return value on success (the current 3 tests never assert the success return value)
All existing tests continue to pass
userStories.notes: File: platform/mcp-server/src/browser-tools/execute-script.test.ts. The test uses mock.module to mock writeExecFile, dispatchToExtension, and deleteExecFile. For the writeExecFile failure test: make the writeExecFile mock reject, then verify the handler rejects and that deleteExecFile is still called (because the handler's finally block runs unconditionally — this is the current behavior and may be worth verifying). For the return value test: the handler returns the result of dispatchToExtension, so mock it to return a specific value and assert the handler returns it.

userStories.title: Add findMatchingTab tests to tab-matching.test.ts
userStories.description: As a platform developer, I want the exported findMatchingTab function to have direct unit tests, so that the function used on every tool dispatch path has verified behavior.
acceptanceCriteria: New describe block for findMatchingTab with at least 3 tests
Test: returns the highest-ranked matching tab when multiple tabs match
Test: returns undefined when no tabs match any pattern
Test: returns undefined when patterns array is empty
All existing tests continue to pass
userStories.notes: File: platform/browser-extension/src/tab-matching.test.ts. The findMatchingTab function is exported from tab-matching.ts and wraps findAllMatchingTabs, returning the first element. The existing test file already has a chrome.tabs.query mock and chrome.windows.getLastFocused mock. Follow the same mock pattern used in the findAllMatchingTabs tests. Import findMatchingTab alongside the existing imports.

userStories.title: Add extension.reload routing tests to message-router.test.ts
userStories.description: As a platform developer, I want the extension.reload handler in the message router to have unit tests, so that every method in the routing table has test coverage.
acceptanceCriteria: New describe block 'extension.reload routing' with at least 2 tests
Test: extension.reload with valid id sends success result and calls chrome.runtime.reload()
Test: extension.reload without id does not send a response
All existing tests continue to pass
userStories.notes: File: platform/browser-extension/src/message-router.test.ts. The source handler is at message-router.ts lines 142-163. It sends a success result, then calls chrome.runtime.reload(). The test file already has mockSendToServer and mockChrome mocks set up. Follow the same pattern as the 'sync.full routing' and 'tool.dispatch routing' test blocks. The chrome.runtime.reload mock needs to be added to the mockChrome setup (it may already exist as a no-op). The handler sends the result via ws (mockSendToServer's second argument).

userStories.title: Add /health and /ws-info response shape tests to http-routes.test.ts
userStories.description: As a platform developer, I want the HTTP route handlers for /health and /ws-info to have unit tests verifying their response shape, so that the API contract is tested at the unit level.
acceptanceCriteria: New describe block for health endpoint handler with tests verifying the response JSON includes: status, uptime, plugins (count and names), extensionConnected, reloadCount, version
New describe block for ws-info endpoint handler with test verifying the response includes the WebSocket URL with auth token
Tests use minimal state fixtures (not a full running server)
All existing tests continue to pass
userStories.notes: File: platform/mcp-server/src/http-routes.test.ts. The source file http-routes.ts exports handler functions that take state and return Response objects. The health handler is a function that reads from state.plugins, state.extensionWs, state.reloadCount, state.startedAt, state.outdatedPlugins, and state.toolConfig. Create a minimal state object using createState() from state.ts, populate the fields needed, call the handler, and verify the JSON response body. For /ws-info, the handler reads the port from the server and state.config.secret. Check http-routes.ts for the exact function signatures and create appropriate test state.

userStories.title: Add disabled tool filtering test to mcp-setup.test.ts
userStories.description: As a platform developer, I want to verify at the unit level that disabled tools are excluded from the MCP tools/list response, so that the tool enable/disable feature has unit-level regression coverage.
acceptanceCriteria: New test in mcp-setup.test.ts that registers a plugin with multiple tools, disables one via state.toolConfig, and verifies the disabled tool is absent from the tools/list response
The test verifies that re-enabling the tool makes it reappear in tools/list
All existing tests continue to pass
userStories.notes: File: platform/mcp-server/src/mcp-setup.test.ts. The tools/list handler is registered by registerMcpHandlers. To test this, you need to create a McpServer instance via createMcpServer(), register handlers with registerMcpHandlers(), set up state with plugins and toolConfig, then exercise the tools/list handler. Look at how the existing rebuildToolLookups tests create state and plugins. The isToolEnabled function in state.ts checks state.toolConfig — set a tool to false there. The MCP Server from @modelcontextprotocol/sdk may need to be called via its request handler. Check the MCP SDK for how to invoke a tools/list request programmatically. If this is too complex to test at the unit level without significant mocking infrastructure, consider adding a simpler test that verifies rebuildToolLookups's cachedBrowserTools or pluginToolLookup correctly reflects the toolConfig state.

userStories.title: Add checkPlugins and checkConfigFile tests to doctor.test.ts
userStories.description: As a platform developer, I want the doctor command's checkPlugins and checkConfigFile functions to have unit tests, so that the primary user diagnostic tool has verified behavior for its most complex checks.
acceptanceCriteria: New describe block for checkConfigFile: test returns pass when config file exists, returns fail when missing

New describe block for checkPlugins with at least 5 tests: no plugins in config returns pass with '0 plugins' message, valid plugin directory returns pass, missing plugin directory returns warn, missing manifest file returns warn, missing IIFE file returns warn
Tests use temp directories for filesystem fixtures
All existing tests continue to pass
userStories.notes: File: platform/cli/src/commands/doctor.test.ts. The source is doctor.ts. checkConfigFile reads from the config path (uses getConfigPath from ../config.js). checkPlugins reads config, iterates plugins array, checks for directory existence, manifest existence, IIFE existence. Both functions return { status: 'pass'|'warn'|'fail', message: string }. The test should set OPENTABS_CONFIG_DIR env var to a temp directory (like other CLI tests do — see cli/config.test.ts for the pattern). For checkPlugins, create a temp config dir with config.json containing plugin paths, and set up the plugin directories (or not) to test each scenario. Functions to export: check if checkPlugins and checkConfigFile are already exported — if not, they may need to be exported for testing. Read the doctor.ts file first to confirm the function signatures.

userStories.title: Add convertToolSchemas tests to build.test.ts
userStories.description: As a plugin developer, I want the Zod-to-JSON-Schema conversion function to have unit tests, so that the documented footgun (transform/pipe/preprocess failures) has regression coverage.
acceptanceCriteria: New describe block for convertToolSchemas with at least 4 tests
Test: valid Zod object schema converts to JSON Schema with correct structure
Test: $schema key is stripped from both input and output schemas
Test: schema using .transform() throws with error message containing 'cannot use .transform()'
Test: valid schema with nested objects/arrays converts correctly
convertToolSchemas is exported from build.ts for testing (if not already)
All existing tests continue to pass
userStories.notes: File: platform/cli/src/commands/build.test.ts. The source function is convertToolSchemas in build.ts lines 71-98. It takes a ToolDefinition (which has .input and .output Zod schemas) and returns { inputSchema, outputSchema }. Currently only validatePlugin is exported — convertToolSchemas needs to be added to the export statement at the bottom of build.ts. The function uses z.toJSONSchema() from Zod 4. For the transform test: create a schema like z.object({ name: z.string().transform(s => s.toUpperCase()) }) and verify it throws. For the valid schema test: use z.object({ name: z.string(), count: z.number().optional() }) and verify the output has the correct JSON Schema structure ({ type: 'object', properties: { name: { type: 'string' }, count: { type: 'number' } } } or similar). The ToolDefinition interface requires name, description, input, output, and handle fields.

userStories.title: Fix imprecise assertions in reload.test.ts
userStories.description: As a platform developer, I want the reload test assertions to use exact counts instead of toBeGreaterThanOrEqual, so that over-calling bugs (e.g., double notification) are caught.
acceptanceCriteria: The assertion at approximately line 193 uses toBe() with the exact expected count instead of toBeGreaterThanOrEqual()
The assertion at approximately line 237 uses toBe() with the exact expected count instead of toBeGreaterThanOrEqual()
All existing tests continue to pass with the stricter assertions
userStories.notes: File: platform/mcp-server/src/reload.test.ts. Search for toBeGreaterThanOrEqual — there are exactly 2 occurrences. The first is in the 're-registers MCP on hot reload' test around line 193 — the expected count should be exactly 2 (one registerMcpHandlers call per session, and there is 1 session in that test — but verify by reading the test setup). The second is in the 'notifies sessions' test around line 237 — the expected count should be exactly 1. Read the test code carefully to determine the exact expected values before changing. Run `bun test platform/mcp-server/src/reload.test.ts` after the change to verify.

userStories.title: Add formatBytes and formatTimestamp tests to build.test.ts
userStories.description: As a platform developer, I want the pure formatting functions in build.ts to have unit tests, so that all extractable pure logic has coverage consistent with the codebase pattern (e.g., formatUptime in status.test.ts).
acceptanceCriteria: New describe block for formatBytes: tests bytes (< 1024), kilobytes (1024-1048575), megabytes (>= 1048576), and boundary values (exactly 1024, exactly 1048576)
New describe block for formatTimestamp: verifies output matches HH:MM:SS format
formatBytes and formatTimestamp are exported from build.ts for testing
All existing tests continue to pass
userStories.notes: File: platform/cli/src/commands/build.test.ts. Source functions are in build.ts: formatBytes (line 165-169) and formatTimestamp (line 171-177). Both are currently not exported — add them to the export statement at the bottom of build.ts. formatBytes has 3 branches: < 1024 returns 'N B', < 1024*1024 returns 'N.N KB', else returns 'N.N MB'. Test each branch plus boundaries (0, 1023, 1024, 1048575, 1048576). formatTimestamp returns HH:MM:SS — use a regex test /^\d{2}:\d{2}:\d{2}$/ since the actual time varies. This follows the same pattern as formatUptime tests in status.test.ts.
